description_long: This is a large, cubic storage box constructed from what appears to be wooden planks, featuring a reinforced frame and diagonal cross-bracing on two of its visible sides. The box has a simple, utilitarian design with a matte, light brown finish, and the wood grain is subtly visible, giving it a sturdy and robust appearance. The top of the box is flat and bordered by a thick frame, with a single diagonal plank running across it for added structural support. There are no visible handles, locks, or hinges, suggesting the lid may be removable or simply set in place. The surfaces are smooth but not glossy, and the box is devoid of any labels, logos, or decorative elements. This type of storage box is commonly found in urban environments such as garages, warehouses, or back alleys, where it is used for storing or transporting goods.
description: A large, cubic wooden storage box with reinforced framing and diagonal cross-bracing, finished in matte light brown. It has a flat top and no visible handles, locks, or labels.
description_view_0: This view shows a plain side of the box with a thick frame and a single diagonal plank on the top surface.
description_view_1: This view highlights a side with a prominent diagonal cross-brace, adding structural detail to the box.
description_view_2: This view displays another plain side similar to view 0, with the top diagonal plank visible.
description_view_3: This view shows the second cross-braced side, mirroring the structural reinforcement seen in view 1.
